extra.message: Unsanitized input from the HTTP request body flows into models.db.session.execute, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.

Unsanitized input from the HTTP request body flows into yaml.load, where it is used to deserialize an object. This may result in an Unsafe Deserialization vulnerability.
Avoid hardcoding values that are meant to be secret. Found a hardcoded string used in here.
A hardcoded string value is used as a cipher key in jwt.decode. Generate the value with a cryptographically strong random number generator instead.
Do not hardcode passwords in code. Found hardcoded password used in a dictionary key.

Running the application in debug mode (debug flag is set to True in run) is a security risk if the application is accessible by untrusted parties.
hashlib.md5 is insecure. Consider changing it to a secure hashing algorithm.

A password from the HTTP request body flows into set_password, without first having django.contrib.auth.password_validation.validate_password called on it. This may allow passwords to be set that don't conform to the application's password policy.
